ops.title: Review follow-ups from the 205faad watch-cursor review
ops.description:
Adversarially-verified findings from the v0.14.0 per-watcher-cursor review (full verdicts: ~/.cache/codex-ask/runs/codex-root.dc_vktdp/interact-refute/codex-r-8c4_edxy). Three keepers, all low severity:
1. Activity.polls grows one entry per watch-<pid> relaunch with no expiry (daemon/activity.go:103). CONFIRMED, ~0.1-0.2 KiB/entry; fix: finite poll-retention horizon pruned opportunistically under Activity.mu.
2. StatusBody's ConsumerConnected and Consumers are computed under separate lock acquisitions (daemon/activity.go:79-99) — reachable inconsistent snapshot. CONFIRMED, very low; fix: one Activity method returning both under a single mu hold.
3. LATENT: watch's pid-suffixed consumer identity (watch-<pid>) breaks any future exact-'watch' caller of MuteConsumer/InjectEvent/Attached/PolledSince (daemon/subscriptions.go:115 is exact-match). Nothing hits it today (cc-present uses 'channel' only; status aggregates the prefix). Guard with doc comments on watchConsumer + Config.MuteConsumer, or a prefix-aware matcher, before any exact-'watch' caller appears.

ops.text: Activity.polls pruning implemented with a test, or declined with rationale in a comment
Connected-flag + counts computed under one lock acquisition, with a test
watchConsumer identity sharp edge documented at watchConsumer and Config.MuteConsumer
